title: Platanthera keiskei
intro: Platanthera keiskei, also known as Keiske's orchid, is a species of orchid native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and boost the immune system. Bioactive constituents of Platanthera keiskei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and other infections in traditional East Asian medicine.
taxonomy: Platanthera keiskei is a species of orchid in the genus Platanthera. It is classified as part of the Orchidaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The genus Platanthera is further classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae, and the family Orchidaceae is a member of the order Asparagales. The order Asparagales is part of the larger class Liliopsida, which includes many flowering plants that have flowers with three petals. Platanthera keiskei's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in these groups.
uses: Platanthera keiskei is known to have potential medicinal uses, particularly in traditional Japanese medicine.
